executive_summary: The system allows users to register with an email and password, requiring email verification before first login. A unique verification link is sent immediately after registration and is valid for 24 hours. Users may request a resend every 60 seconds. Password complexity rules include uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special characters, with restrictions on consecutive spaces and inclusion of email prefix or username. Email uniqueness and format validation are enforced. The email verification depends on an external delivery service, posing risks of delays or failures.
functional_summary: Users can create an account using an email and password (FR001). Immediately after registration, the system sends a unique verification link via email (FR002). Users can request a resend of the verification link every 60 seconds (FR003). The system requires email verification before the first login (FR004).
confirmed_business_rules: Email must be unique across all accounts (BR001).
The verification link sent via email is valid for 24 hours (BR002).
The password must contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one numeric digit, and one special character (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *) (BR003).
Consecutive spaces in the password are forbidden (BR004).
The password cannot contain the user's explicit email prefix or username (BR005).
validation_rules: Email format must contain '@' and a domain (VAL001).
Password length must be between 8 and 12 characters inclusive (VAL002).
When a user tries to register with an already existing email, the system shall display a meaningful inline error message (VAL003).
open_questions: What is the exact list of allowed special characters for the password? (MISS001)
What is the maximum number of resend requests for the verification link? (MISS002)
What is the behavior after the verification link expires (e.g., can the user request a new link?) beyond the resend mechanism? (MISS003)
assumptions: The password length boundaries (8 and 12 characters) are inclusive as confirmed by the answer to Q005.
The special characters listed in the business rule (e.g., @, #, $, %, ^, &, *) are considered allowed, but the exact set is not fully specified.
The 'meaningful inline error' for duplicate email registration will be defined by the development team or UX design.
risks: Email delivery delays or failures could prevent users from completing registration and logging in (RISK001).
The 24-hour expiry of the verification link may cause user frustration if the link is not used in time (RISK002).
The external email delivery service dependency (DEP001) introduces potential availability and reliability issues.
